task_completion.description: Draw.io application is open and used to create the diagram
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io (diagrams.net) interface open with a canvas being edited.

task_completion.description: All 7 required stations are present and labeled
task_completion.visual_evidence: The canvas contains text labels for 'South Station', 'Downtown Crossing', 'Park Street', 'State', 'Government Center', 'Haymarket', and 'North Station'.

task_completion.description: Red Line connections are drawn correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line colored red connects the nodes for South Station, Downtown Crossing, and Park Street in that sequence.

task_completion.description: Orange Line connections are drawn correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line colored orange connects the nodes for Downtown Crossing, State, Haymarket, and North Station in that sequence.

task_completion.description: Blue Line connections are drawn correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line colored blue connects the nodes for Government Center and State.

task_completion.description: Green Line connections are drawn correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line colored green connects the nodes for Park Street, Government Center, Haymarket, and North Station in that sequence.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved and exported to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Desktop or a file explorer window shows the files 'mbta_map.drawio' and 'mbta_map.png', or a save/export dialog confirms these exact filenames and locations.

integrity.description: Agent used the draw.io GUI to construct the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of mouse clicks, shape selection, line drawing, or typing within the draw.io interface, rather than terminal commands generating the files directly.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply download an existing MBTA map
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagram appears to be a custom schematic created with standard draw.io shapes, text boxes, and connectors, rather than a pasted image of the official MBTA map.

integrity.description: The application used is genuinely draw.io
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches draw.io (diagrams.net), including the shape panel on the left, format panel on the right, and standard menu bar.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a schematic map of the Boston MBTA downtown network. Verified colors: Red (#DA291C), Orange (#ED8B00), Blue (#003DA5), Green (#00843D). Verified connections: Red Line (South Station ↔ Downtown Crossing ↔ Park Street); Orange Line (Downtown Crossing ↔ State ↔ Haymarket ↔ North Station); Blue Line (Government Center ↔ State); Green Line (Park Street ↔ Government Center ↔ Haymarket ↔ North Station). All 7 stations must be present. The diagram should be created in draw.io and saved to the Desktop as mbta_map.drawio and mbta_map.png.
